filters.notes: kQ1ShipdateMax = 10471 (1998-09-02 days since epoch). Zone map prunes trailing ~12 blocks. Fast path skips per-row check for blocks where zone_max[b] <= 10471 (~588 of 600 blocks).

scan.zone_map_usage: Read both zone_min[b] and zone_max[b] arrays. Three-way block classification: (1) zone_min[b] > 10471 => skip block entirely; (2) zone_max[b] <= 10471 => fast path (all rows qualify, no per-row date check, #pragma omp simd); (3) otherwise => slow path with per-row date guard.

data_structures.aggregation: direct_array[6] with double accumulators — key = l_returnflag * 2 + l_linestatus; 6 slots; one alignas(64) thread-local copy per thread; reduce by summation after parallel scan
data_structures.accumulator_type: double (NOT long double) — eliminates x87 FPU register spilling; 5 doubles + int64_t = 48 bytes/slot; 6 slots = 288 bytes/thread; total 64 threads = 18 KB, fits in L2
data_structures.fast_path_inner_loop: When zone_max[b] <= 10471: inner loop has no date branch. Use #pragma omp simd to vectorize per-row arithmetic (disc_price, charge) and accumulation. Compiler may generate AVX-512 code for the double arithmetic.
data_structures.simd_strategy: For the fast-path block loop: #pragma omp simd reduction applied per accumulator variable (unroll by group key if needed). For the slow-path block loop: keep scalar with per-row date guard as before.

parallelism.schedule: static — with 600 blocks / 64 threads each thread gets ~9 consecutive blocks, improving hardware prefetcher effectiveness vs dynamic per-block dispatch
parallelism.omp_warmup: Inside data_loading phase, BEFORE mmap_prefetch_all: fire a single dummy '#pragma omp parallel {}' to force OMP thread pool creation (creates 64 OS threads). This absorbs the ~34ms OMP init cost into data_loading, where it overlaps with file open and madvise system calls. After warmup, omp_get_max_threads() is instant and the main_scan parallel region launches immediately.

indexes.purpose: Three-way block classification: skip (zone_min > threshold), fast_path (zone_max <= threshold, no per-row check), slow_path (boundary blocks with per-row check)

output.sort_strategy: emit slots 0..5 in key order; skip slots with count == 0; no runtime sort needed since key = returnflag*2+linestatus is already ascending

decode.l_returnflag: rf_chars[] = {'A','N','R'} — index by dict code (A=0, N=1, R=2 from encode_returnflag)
decode.l_linestatus: ls_chars[] = {'F','O'} — index by dict code (F=0, O=1 from encode_linestatus)

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category A + C): Two compounding issues in iter_0. First, long double accumulators force x87 FPU usage; with 30 accumulators (5 per slot × 6 slots) vs only 8 x87 registers, the compiler spills 22 accumulators to stack every iteration, generating ~352 extra bytes of memory traffic per row on top of the 38 bytes of column data — this alone roughly doubles effective bandwidth pressure. Switching to double eliminates the spilling, fits 6 slots comfortably in YMM/ZMM registers, and enables AVX-512 vectorization of the arithmetic. Second, the current code never loads zone_max from the zone map, so ALL passing blocks go through the scalar slow path with a per-row date branch; adding the three-way classification (skip/fast-path/slow-path) allows ~588 of 600 blocks to use a branch-free #pragma omp simd inner loop. Third, the ~34ms unaccounted gap in iter_0 is OMP thread pool creation (64 OS threads on first omp_get_max_threads() call, outside any measured phase); firing a dummy parallel region inside data_loading absorbs this cost into a phase that already waits for mmap/madvise syscalls. Combined, these changes target eliminating the x87 spill overhead and enabling SIMD vectorization, with the goal of bringing main_scan from 40ms closer to the memory-bandwidth limit (~20ms at ~55% DRAM efficiency on this NUMA system).
